image:image.image:caption: Fan coil unit FCU HVAC electrical symbol BS EN 60617

image:image.image:title: Electric Towel Rail Symbol
image:image.image:caption: Electric towel rail bathroom electrical symbol BS EN 60617

image:image.image:title: Heater (General) Symbol
image:image.image:caption: Electric heater electrical symbol BS EN 60617

image:image.image:title: Hand Dryer Symbol
image:image.image:caption: Electric hand dryer electrical symbol BS EN 60617

image:image.image:title: Solar PV Panel Symbol
image:image.image:caption: Solar photovoltaic PV panel electrical symbol BS EN 60617

image:image.image:title: PV Inverter Symbol
image:image.image:caption: Solar PV inverter DC-AC electrical symbol BS EN 60617

image:image.image:title: Battery Storage Symbol
image:image.image:caption: Battery energy storage system BESS electrical symbol BS EN 60617

image:image.image:title: EV Distribution Board Symbol
image:image.image:caption: EV charger distribution board electrical symbol BS EN 60617

image:image.image:title: Generator Symbol
image:image.image:caption: Generator backup power electrical symbol BS EN 60617

image:image.image:title: BMS Controller Symbol
image:image.image:caption: Building management system BMS controller electrical symbol BS EN 60617

image:image.image:title: Control Panel Symbol
image:image.image:caption: Electrical control panel electrical symbol BS EN 60617

image:image.image:title: Lighting Control Module Symbol
image:image.image:caption: Lighting control module DALI KNX electrical symbol BS EN 60617

image:image.image:title: Generic Sensor Symbol
image:image.image:caption: BMS sensor occupancy temperature electrical symbol BS EN 60617

image:image.image:title: Humidity Sensor Symbol
image:image.image:caption: Humidity sensor electrical symbol BS EN 60617

image:image.image:title: Door (Hinged Left) Symbol
image:image.image:caption: Door left-hinged architectural symbol electrical drawing

image:image.image:title: Door (Hinged Right) Symbol
image:image.image:caption: Door right-hinged architectural symbol electrical drawing

image:image.image:title: Double Door Symbol
image:image.image:caption: Double door architectural symbol electrical drawing

image:image.image:title: Window Symbol
image:image.image:caption: Window architectural symbol electrical drawing

image:image.image:title: Stairs Symbol
image:image.image:caption: Stairs architectural symbol electrical drawing

image:image.image:title: North Arrow Symbol
image:image.image:caption: North arrow compass orientation drawing symbol

image:image.image:title: RCD (Residual Current Device) — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A Residual Current Device to BS EN 61008. Detects imbalance between live and neutral (earth-fault leakage) and trips. 30 mA RCDs provide additional protection against electric shock.

image:image.image:title: RCBO (RCD + MCB Combined) — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A Residual Current Breaker with Overcurrent protection to BS EN 61009. Combines RCD + MCB in one device. Each circuit has individual earth fault and overload protection.

image:image.image:title: MCB (Miniature Circuit Breaker) — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A Miniature Circuit Breaker to BS EN 60898. Protects against overload and short-circuit fault current. Types B, C and D differentiate by magnetic trip characteristic.

image:image.image:title: SPD (Surge Protection Device) — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A Surge Protective Device to BS EN 61643-11. Type 1 (lightning current), Type 2 (transient overvoltage), Type 3 (point of use). BS 7671 443.4 risk assessment usually requires Type 2 at origin.

image:image.image:title: Two-Way Switch — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A switch with two fixed contacts and one common, allowing control of the same load from two locations. Always used in pairs with a second 2-way switch (e.g. staircase, hall-and-landing).

image:image.image:title: One-Way Switch — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A single switch that breaks or makes the live conductor to a load from one position. The most common domestic switch — used for a single lighting circuit controlled from one location.

image:image.image:title: Intermediate Switch — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A four-terminal switch wired between two 2-way switches to give control from three or more locations. The strappers cross over inside the switch.

image:image.image:title: Dimmer Switch — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A switch with a variable resistor or electronic dimming module that controls light output. Specify type — leading-edge for incandescent, trailing-edge or LED-rated for modern LED drivers.

image:image.image:title: Double 13A Socket Outlet — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A twin 13A switched socket — two outlets on one back box. Drawn as two semicircles back-to-back. Standard outlet for living spaces, bedrooms and offices.

image:image.image:title: Single 13A Socket Outlet — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A single 13A switched socket to BS 1363. The basic socket symbol — a semicircle with a line indicating switched. UK standard for general-purpose socket circuits.

image:image.image:title: Fused Spur (Unswitched) — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A Fused Connection Unit (FCU) with internal fuse providing local protection for a fixed appliance. Unswitched version — no front rocker. Used where switching is not needed at the spur.

image:image.image:title: Switched Fused Spur — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A Fused Connection Unit with a front-panel switch + neon indicator. Allows local isolation of the fixed appliance without resorting to the consumer unit. Most common FCU type.

image:image.image:title: Isolator (Switch-Disconnector) — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A switching device that fully isolates a circuit or item of equipment for maintenance. Must be lockable in the OFF position per BS 7671 Section 537. Different from a functional switch.

image:image.image:title: Emergency Stop (E-Stop) — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A latching push-button that immediately disconnects supply to dangerous equipment. Mushroom head, red on yellow background. Must be reset deliberately — twist-release or key-reset.

image:image.image:title: Smoke Detector — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: An optical or ionisation smoke detector to BS EN 14604 (single-station, domestic) or BS 5839-1 (fire alarm system). Interlinked types signal one another via wire or RF.

image:image.image:title: Fire Alarm Sounder — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A fire alarm sounder to BS EN 54-3. Produces minimum 65 dB at the bedhead per BS 5839-1, with the fire-alarm tone defined in BS 5839 Annex E.

image:image.image:title: Manual Call Point (Break Glass) — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A manual call point to BS EN 54-11. The break-glass/press-glass element used to manually trigger the fire alarm system. Sited near exits and on escape routes.

image:image.image:title: Exit Sign — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A maintained or non-maintained emergency exit sign to BS EN 1838. Indicates the direction of escape; runs on battery during mains failure for at least 3 hours per BS 5266.

image:image.image:title: Emergency Light — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A non-maintained emergency light to BS 5266. Off in normal use; switches on automatically when the mains supply fails. Tested monthly and annually per BS 5266-1.

image:image.image:title: Consumer Unit — BS EN 60617 Electrical Symbol
image:image.image:caption: A domestic consumer unit (fuseboard) to BS EN 61439-3. Houses the main switch, RCD/RCBO/MCB protective devices, and (since 2018) SPD and AFDD where required.
